metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the proposed spy targets (sign_manifest, transition, reopen, event-append) are actually reachable from the three floor functions; if not, either narrow the claim (the floor is side-effect-free by construction, not by spy-catching) or widen the test scope to the entry point that actually calls both the floor and the signing path.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that 'sign_manifest / transition / reopen / event-append' are viable spy targets when running the floor (the three floor functions) over the _partial_epic fixture. A grep of the three floor functions (_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict, _classify_completion, _maybe_apply_completion_floor in plan_review/__init__.py and passes.py) shows that none of sign_manifest, transition, or reopen( appear in those call paths — sign_manifest is only called from attest.py (outside the floor), and reopen/transition are not called anywhere in the plan_review module. Spies placed on these symbols while invoking only the floor functions will show zero calls trivially, providing no stronger guarantee than the source-token test they replace.
findings.location: ## What / Acceptance Criteria (AC1: 'Spies assert zero calls to signing/transition/reopen/append')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC2: specify exactly what 'store + attestation snapshots' means in this test context (e.g., the in-memory verdict dict is byte-identical before/after a zero-drop run, or no _seam.append_event is called) so the implementer knows what to assert.
findings.finding: AC2 says 'Before/after store + attestation snapshots are unchanged' but the current test has NO store or attestation snapshots — it only checks source tokens and _MANIFEST_PREFIX. AC2 implicitly introduces NEW snapshot assertions that don't exist today, without specifying what is snapshotted (which store path, which attestation fields) or how.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC2 with: 'The in-memory verdict dict is byte-identical before/after a no-drop floor run, and no rebar._commands._seam.append_event call is recorded by the spy.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact import binding(s) to patch for each spy (e.g., `rebar.signing.sign_manifest` at the module where the floor functions resolve their imports) and add an adversarial smoke-check — temporarily inject a `sign_manifest(...)` call into the floor, confirm the test goes RED, then revert — to prove AC3's 'even via a helper/rename' guarantee is actually enforced.
findings.finding: AC3 states the spy test 'fails if a signing/transition call is introduced even via a helper/rename', but the plan never specifies WHICH import binding(s) to patch. Python monkeypatching only intercepts the specific binding being patched; a floor helper that imports `sign_manifest` via a different path (e.g., `rebar.signing.sign_manifest` vs. `rebar.sign_manifest` vs. `attest.sign_plan_review`) is invisible to a spy on the wrong binding. This is the same evasion class the plan set out to fix, moved one layer deeper — the spy mechanism only achieves AC3 if the patch target is the binding the production code actually resolves at call time.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Test fails if a signing/transition call is introduced (even via a helper/rename).'
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's What or AC section, add: 'Patch sign_manifest at the binding the floor module imports from (e.g., `rebar.signing` or `rebar.llm.plan_review.attest`) so any call through a helper is intercepted. Verify binding correctness with a one-shot adversarial injection: add a bare `signing.sign_manifest("x", [])` call inside `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict`, run the new test, confirm RED, remove the injection.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving step for AC3: inject a deliberate signing/transition call into the fixture (e.g., a one-line mutation of `_partial_epic` or a temporary monkeypatch wrapper that calls `sign_manifest`) and assert that `make test` (or `pytest -k test_non_regression_floor_is_review_only`) reports a FAILURE, then revert — document this as an adversarial smoke-check command in the plan.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Test fails if a signing/transition call is introduced') has no mapped step or proving command that demonstrates the RED behavior. An executor who writes passing spies but accidentally writes them incorrectly (e.g., patches the wrong import path) would satisfy AC1+AC4 while AC3 silently fails — the plan provides no command to evidence the RED path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Test fails if a signing/transition call is introduced (even via a helper/rename).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC step such as: 'Adversarial check: temporarily add `sign_manifest(...)` inside the floor function (or a helper it calls), run `pytest tests/unit/test_completion_floor_e2e.py::test_non_regression_floor_is_review_only` and confirm it exits non-zero, then revert.' This can be a manual smoke step or a dedicated xfail sub-test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `append_event` and file-write spy targets are reachable from the three floor functions before wiring those spies; if not, drop them from AC1 to avoid dead scaffolding.
findings.finding: The plan specifies spies on 'event-append / file writes', but the three floor functions under test (_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict, _classify_completion, _maybe_apply_completion_floor in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py lines 229-357) contain no calls to append_event or file-write operations. Installing spies for call paths that don't exist in the functions under test is unnecessary scaffolding (YAGNI).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria line 1: 'Spies assert zero calls to signing/transition/reopen/append and no store mutation escapes the returned verdict dict.'
findings.suggested_fix: Narrow AC1 to the confirmed reachable targets: `sign_manifest`, `transition`, and `reopen`. Drop `append_event` / file-write spies unless a code path from the floor functions to those calls can be identified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly address the `rebar.llm.completion` source-scan assertion (lines 192-195): state whether it is kept as-is, converted to a different behavioral check, or intentionally dropped with a rationale.
findings.finding: The existing test_non_regression_floor_is_review_only contains a third assertion — `assert 'completion_floor' not in inspect.getsource(rebar.llm.completion)` — that checks an *unrelated module* (the close/verify path) for source-token absence. This is not replaceable by a spy on floor functions and is neither addressed nor mentioned in the plan's replacement strategy.
findings.location: What / Acceptance Criteria AC1 — test_non_regression_floor_is_review_only lines 192-195

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact import paths / callable names for 'event-append' and 'file writes' spies (e.g., `epic_store.append_event`, `attestation_store.write`) so an executor can act without investigation.
findings.finding: 'event-append' and 'file writes' in AC1/What section are not identified by module path or callable name, leaving an executor to guess which functions to spy on.
findings.location: What section: 'monkeypatch spies around `sign_manifest` / `transition` / `reopen` / event-append / file writes'; AC1: 'no store mutation escapes the returned verdict dict'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that explicitly verifies the `inspect.getsource` / source-token assertions (`sign_manifest`, `transition`, `reopen(` token checks and the `:181-198` source check) are removed from `test_completion_floor_e2e.py`.
findings.finding: The plan is a migration (replace source-token assertions with spy assertions), but no Acceptance Criterion explicitly verifies that the OLD source-token / inspect.getsource assertions are removed. AC1–AC3 only verify the new spy behavior, leaving removal unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four items) vs. 'What' section ('Replace the source-token assertions')
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] `inspect.getsource`-based token assertions and the `:181-198` source-range check are deleted from `test_non_regression_floor_is_review_only`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC1 whether 'no store mutation' includes file-write spies, or add a separate AC line explicitly asserting zero file-write calls (matching the 'file writes' spy listed in 'What').
findings.finding: The 'What' section lists 'file writes' as one of the spy targets, but no Acceptance Criterion names or verifies a spy on file writes specifically; AC1 covers it only obliquely via 'no store mutation escapes the returned verdict dict', which may or may not encompass file-write side effects depending on implementation.
findings.location: 'What' section ('event-append / file writes') vs. AC1 ('no store mutation escapes the returned verdict dict')
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC1 to: 'Spies assert zero calls to signing/transition/reopen/append/file-writes and no in-memory store mutation escapes the returned verdict dict.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
